articles.title: India temporarily blocks Telegram over medical exam cheating fears
articles.summary: Authorities said scammers previously exploited the feature by posting fake exam questions before the test and later replacing them with the real questions, making it look like they had leaked the exam in advance.

articles.title: iRhythm Confirms Data Stolen in Hack
articles.summary: The digital health company said it learned of the breach on June 8 and the attackers demanded a ransom. The post iRhythm Confirms Data Stolen in Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: UK to require ID or face scan before you can make social media accounts
articles.summary: Opening a new social media account in the UK will soon mean proving you're over 16 with an ID upload or a facial age scan, under a government ban on under-16s taking effect in spring 2027. Security experts warn the age checks are easy to circumvent and create new data-breach risks. [...]

articles.title: Hacker Conversations: Isira Adithya, the Evolution of an Ethical Hacker
articles.summary: From building LED bulbs to graduating college and buying a house with money earned from bug bounties. The post Hacker Conversations: Isira Adithya, the Evolution of an Ethical Hacker appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: GhostTree Attack Abused Recursive Windows Junctions to Hide Malware
articles.summary: GhostTree uses recursive NTFS junctions to generate vast numbers of valid Windows file paths. Varonis explains how the technique could cause Microsoft Defender folder scans to never complete, leaving malware undetected. [...]

articles.title: AI and Cybersecurity – Everything You Wanted to Know, But Were Afraid to Ask
articles.summary: From defending networks to enabling attacks, artificial intelligence is changing every aspect of cybersecurity. articles.summary: Security researchers at Zimperium's zLabs have documented a new Android banking trojan, Rokarolla, that targets 217 banking and cryptocurrency apps and packs 137 remote commands. articles.title: Cybercrime Group Claims Novo Nordisk Hack
articles.summary: The hack-and-leak group FulcrumSec claims to have stolen 1.3TB of data from the pharmaceutical giant. The post Cybercrime Group Claims Novo Nordisk Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: California Water Service says there is no indication of operational disruptions to its water and wastewater systems. The post Cal Water Investigating Iranian Hackers’ Claims appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Flock Cameras Are Being Used for Stalking
articles.summary: There are over a dozen cases around the country where police officers are using the Flock surveillance camera system to obsessively and illegally stalk people. Alternate link.

articles.title: Atomic Arch Supply Chain Attack Hits 1,500 AUR Packages
articles.summary: Arch Linux suspended account registrations in response to the wave of malicious packages being uploaded to AUR. The post Atomic Arch Supply Chain Attack Hits 1,500 AUR Packages appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA warns of another cPanel plugin flaw exploited in attacks
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has given U.S. government agencies three days to secure their servers against an actively exploited vulnerability (CVE-2026-54420) in the LiteSpeed cPanel user-end plugin. [...]

articles.summary: Bad actors are exploiting multiple security vulnerabilities in Fortinet FortiSandbox, according to threat intelligence firm Defused Cyber. articles.title: Ransomware gang abuses Microsoft Teams relays to hide malicious traffic
articles.summary: DragonForce ransomware used a custom malware named 'Backdoor.Turn' to hide command-and-control traffic inside Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure. [...]

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two previously undocumented Windows variants of what was believed to be a Linux-only backdoor called SprySOCKS. articles.title: Critical Fortinet FortiSandbox flaws now exploited in attacks
articles.summary: Attackers are now exploiting several critical vulnerabilities in Fortinet's FortiSandbox cyber threat detection platform, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]

articles.title: Windows version of SprySOCKS Linux malware used to attack govt orgs
articles.summary: Windows variants for the SprySOCKS Linux malware have been used in attacks targeting government organizations in at least four countries. [...]

articles.summary: The North Korean state-sponsored hacking group known as ScarCruft (aka APT37) has been observed using spear-phishing messages impersonating Microsoft Account security notifications to deliver malware called NarwhalRAT. articles.title: iRhythm discloses data breach, says hackers stole patient info
articles.summary: Digital healthcare company iRhythm Holdings has disclosed a data breach after hackers stole patients' personal and health information stored on third-party-hosted business applications. [...]

articles.title: Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks
articles.summary: Cisco recently became aware of the exploitation of CVE-2026-20262, a Catalyst SD-WAN Manager zero-day that allows arbitrary file write. The post Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Cisco has released security updates for a medium-severity security flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that has come under active exploitation in the wild. articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a security flaw impacting LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by June 18, 2026. articles.title: SimpleHelp bug lets hackers create rogue remote support accounts
articles.summary: A vulnerability in the SimpleHelp remote management software allows unauthenticated attackers to create privileged technician accounts on servers using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication protocol. [...]

articles.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
articles.summary: A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email. The way in was a backdoor on their REDCap research servers that stole login credentials. The exfiltration was the un

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two malicious cyber campaigns that exhibit similarities with a persistent North Korean threat cluster known as Contagious Interview (aka Famous Chollima, HexagonalRodent, and Void Dokkaebi). According to a report published by Proofpoint, the threat actor has be

articles.title: Maine closes data breach portal to the public after fake reports
articles.summary: Maine is still allowing companies to report breaches, but won’t make the portal easily available to the public until after it completes an audit of its procedures to stop such incidents, according to a press release from the Maine attorney general’s office.

articles.title: OptinMonster WordPress plugin hacked in CDN supply-chain attack
articles.summary: WordPress plugins OptinMonster, TrustPulse, and PushEngage have been compromised in a supply-chain attack impacting Awesome Motive-s content distribution network (CDN). [...]

articles.title: Cisco fixes SD-WAN vManage flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
articles.summary: Cisco has released security updates to address a vulnerability in the Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, that was exploited in attacks to escalate to root privileges. [...]

articles.summary: A default low-privilege account on a LiteLLM proxy can climb to full admin and run code on the server by chaining three vulnerabilities, researchers at Obsidian Security disclosed LiteLLM is a widely deployed open-source AI gateway that brokers calls to more than 100 model providers behind one OpenA

articles.title: Council of Europe investigates ShinyHunters data breach claims
articles.summary: The Council of Europe, the continent's oldest intergovernmental body, is probing claims of a data breach made by the ShinyHunters extortion group over the weekend. [...]

articles.title: Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer
articles.summary: Mackay Sugar was targeted in a cyberattack carried out by a threat group known as The Gentlemen. The post Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: A single click on a trusted Microsoft link could have let an attacker pull emails, calendar details, and indexed files out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search. Researchers at Varonis Threat Labs chained three bugs into a one-click exfiltration path they call SearchLeak. Because the link point

articles.title: Cyberattack on Russian tech firm Astral disrupts business, government services for week
articles.summary: According to customer complaints, the disruption affected a range of services used by businesses, leading to interruptions in cash register operations, difficulties selling certain regulated goods, loss of access to customer portals and corporate email and problems with electronic human resources do

articles.title: Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America
articles.summary: Google’s Threat Intelligence Group has been tracking the cyberespionage group as UNC6508 since early 2025. The post Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Chinese hackers breach REDCap servers, steal medical research
articles.summary: A China-linked espionage campaign targeted exposed REDCap servers to deploy the InfiniteRed malware and steal sensitive data from a medical institution in North America. [...]

articles.title: New attack turned Microsoft 365 Copilot into 1-click data theft tool
articles.summary: A critical vulnerability chain dubbed SearchLeak in Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise could allow attackers to steal sensitive data from a target's mailbox, OneDrive, or SharePoint account through a specially crafted URL. [...]

articles.title: Infinite Campus data breach affects 137,000 school staff accounts
articles.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from more than 137,000 school staff accounts in a Salesforce data theft attack that targeted the widely used Infinite Campus K-12 student information system in March. [...]

articles.title: Webinar: How behavioral AI stops phishing and account takeovers
articles.summary: Modern phishing, BEC, and account takeover attacks increasingly bypass traditional email defenses and create operational strain for security teams. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help automate detection, investigation, and remediation to reduce alert fatigue and accelerate response time

articles.title: Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges
articles.summary: Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko admitted to working on the development of a loader for the Conti gang. The post Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems
articles.summary: The pharmaceutical giant says the attackers gained access to personal data stored on the compromised systems. The post Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker
articles.summary: French officials say roughly 73,000 government accounts were affected, while the threat actor claims to have stolen messages and user data from the sovereign Tchap platform. The post French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack
articles.summary: The extortion group threatens to leak 297 GB of data allegedly stolen from the Council of Europe, including employee personal information. The post ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
